title: VOA — Instincts, Yellowstone, Workplace Wellbeing, Mars Geology
content: Section 1: gut reactions idioms · Section 2: Yellowstone National Park · Section 3: saying no at work · Section 4: why Mars is red
attribution: Audio courtesy of VOA Learning English (learningenglish.voanews.com) — public domain. Questions © IELTS Band AI.
license: public domain (US federal government work, commercial reuse permitted with attribution)

questions.question: The two main expressions discussed in this lesson are 'knee-jerk' and 'gut ________'.
The idiom 'knee-jerk' originally comes from a ________ reflex.
A doctor produces the knee-jerk reflex by giving a light blow on the tendon below the ________.
What does the host say a 'knee-jerk' reaction usually means?
options: B. An automatic and often poorly considered reaction
C. A reaction based on long experience
D. A friendly response to bad news
questions.answer: B. An automatic and often poorly considered reaction

questions.question: The host explains that a 'gut reaction' is described as an ________ feeling.
According to the host, the expression 'knee-jerk' is usually used to describe whom?
How does the host say we typically use the phrase 'gut reaction'?
The host's personal story takes place during a hike in the state of ________.
After leaving the trail, the host later learned that a dangerous ________ had been seen in the area.
Which word best matches the meaning of 'knee-jerk' as used in the episode?
Yellowstone was established as the world's first national park in the year ________.
Most of Yellowstone National Park is located in the U.S. state of ________.
About how large is Yellowstone National Park?
The park sits on top of an active super volcano known as the Yellowstone ________.
How many geysers does the park contain, according to the speaker?
The most famous geyser in the park is called ________ Faithful.
About how often does the most famous geyser erupt?
Yellowstone Canyon is about ________ metres deep.
Which large mammal lives in the park in a group of about 4,500 — the largest such group in the world?
Which of the following is NOT mentioned about Yellowstone in the talk?
options: A. It had major forest fires in 1988
B. Its average elevation is about 2,400 metres
C. Its Lower Falls drops 93 metres
D. It is the largest national park in the world
questions.answer: D. It is the largest national park in the world

questions.question: The feature opens with the story of Justin Stewart, who works as a news production ________.
Why was Justin Stewart hospitalised?
options: A. A car accident at work

questions.question: What did Justin's doctor tell him he would have to do?
options: B. Sleep eight hours a night

questions.question: Bobby Dutton of GBM6 says he plans his hardest task every Monday at ________.
What phrase does Amber Krasinski of IvyHill Strategies use to soften a refusal at work?
The New York psychotherapist quoted in the feature is named Israa ________.
What trick does the psychotherapist use to stop checking her work email on weekends?
options: A. She deletes the Gmail app every Friday
B. She moves the Gmail app to her phone's second screen
C. She turns off her phone
D. She uses a separate phone for work
questions.answer: B. She moves the Gmail app to her phone's second screen

questions.question: Which of the following is recommended in the feature as a way to set workplace limits?
options: A. Replying to every email within five minutes
B. Using your email signature to share working hours and holidays
D. Accepting every new project offered
questions.answer: B. Using your email signature to share working hours and holidays

questions.question: According to the experts, what should you do before answering a new work request?
options: B. Ask a colleague to answer for you

questions.question: The slang term used in the feature for working rapidly and tirelessly is ________.
Mars is often called the ________ Planet because of its colour.
The new study suggests Mars's red colour comes mainly from a mineral called ________.
The mineral that was previously thought to give Mars its colour is ________.
Which universities led the international research team?
options: A. Harvard University and University of Cambridge
B. Brown University and University of Bern
C. MIT and University of Oxford
D. Stanford University and ETH Zurich
questions.answer: B. Brown University and University of Bern

questions.question: In which journal were the findings published?
The lead researcher quoted in the feature is Adomas ________.
How was the research carried out?
options: A. By using only telescope images from Earth
B. By combining spacecraft data with synthetic Martian dust made in a laboratory
C. By analysing rocks brought back from the Moon
D. By simulating Mars on a computer only
questions.answer: B. By combining spacecraft data with synthetic Martian dust made in a laboratory

questions.question: Ferrihydrite forms when iron reacts with oxygen and ________.
What does the discovery suggest about Mars's past?
options: A. Mars once had large amounts of liquid water
B. Mars never had any water
C. Mars was once warmer than Earth
D. Mars had an atmosphere thicker than Earth's
questions.answer: A. Mars once had large amounts of liquid water

questions.question: Final confirmation of the result will come from samples being collected by NASA's ________ rover.
